behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names the target file tests/docker_scripts/stress_runner.sh, the cannot_allocate_thread_injection.xml marker, and references start_server in stress_tests.lib; tests key on these same identifiers.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes the problem (date-based logic, need longer timeout, add comment) without specifying $RANDOM or the exact number 30.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests accept any numeric timeout > 6 and only check that $RANDOM-style randomization is used (not date-based), which matches any reasonable fix.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests execute bash subprocesses (bash -n, shellcheck, echo $((RANDOM % 2))) to verify behavior, not just text matching.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests require a numeric timeout > 6 on the post-stress start_server call and non-date randomization; a stub wouldn't pass.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Randomization fix and extended timeout are both tested; comment requirement is not strictly tested but is a minor auxiliary.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are individually named and documented with docstrings explaining purpose.

no_typos.reason: Paths and filenames are consistent between instruction, tests, and solution.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction points to the general area (post-stress restart after removing cannot_allocate_thread_injection.xml) but does not name exact line or exact replacement.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: Code/script edit task, not a config-edit task.
